channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [BANKBARODA] Bank of Baroda Reports 13.93% YoY Growth in Global Business in March 2026
item.description:
Bank of Baroda has reported a YoY growth of 13.93% in its global business, reaching ₹ 30.79 trillion as of 31st March 2026. The bank's global advances grew by 16.23% to ₹ 14.30 trillion, and global deposits increased by 12.00% to ₹ 16.49 trillion during the same period. The growth was consistent across various segments, including domestic deposits and advances.

  • Global Business grew by 13.93% YoY to ₹ 30.79 trillion
  • Global Advances grew by 16.23% YoY to ₹ 14.30 trillion
  • Global Deposits grew by 12.00% YoY to ₹ 16.49 trillion
  • Domestic Deposits grew by 12.83% YoY to ₹ 14,01,497
  • Domestic Advances grew by 14.56% YoY to ₹ 11,69,804

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BANK OF BARODA | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [DELHIVERY] Delhivery Ltd Grants 58,250 Stock Options Under ESOP 2012 to Eligible Employees
item.description:
Delhivery Ltd, in accordance with Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, has approved the grant of 58,250 stock options under Delhivery Employees Stock Option Plan 2012 (ESOP 2012) to eligible employees. The options, each convertible into one fully paid-up equity share with a face value of Re. 1/-, will vest in stages over a period of 4 years from the date of grant. The exercise price is set at Re. 1/- per share.

  • Grant of 58,250 stock options under ESOP 2012 to eligible employees
  • Each stock option is convertible into one fully paid-up equity share with a face value of Re. 1/-
  • Options to vest in stages over a period of 4 years from the date of grant
  • Exercise price set at Re. 1/- per share
  • Delhivery Employees Stock Option Plan 2012 in compliance with SEBI regulations

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Delhivery Ltd | Category: Investment

item.title: [HEXT] Hexaware Technologies Ltd: Director Changes, AGM, and Committee Reconstitution
item.description:
Hexaware Technologies Ltd announced that its Board, at a meeting held on April 02, 2026, approved several decisions. The 33rd Annual General Meeting (AGM) of the members of the Company will be held on Tuesday, May 05, 2026, through video conferencing/other audio-visual means. Mr. Milind Sarwate, Independent Director, will cease to be a Director of the Company upon completion of his second consecutive term. Mr. Alok Chandra Misra has been appointed as his successor. The Board also approved the reconstitution of Committees, with Mr. Alok Chandra Misra and Ms. Sukanya Kripalu appointed as Chairpersons of several Committees.

  • 33rd Annual General Meeting (AGM) on May 05, 2026
  • Mr. Milind Sarwate ceasing to be a Director
  • Appointment of Mr. Alok Chandra Misra as Independent Director
  • Reconstitution of Committees of the Board
  • Appointment of Ms. Sukanya Kripalu as Chairperson of the Nomination & Remuneration Committee

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hexaware Technologies Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [HEXT] Hexaware Technologies Ltd: AGM Date Fixed, Director Milind Sarwate to Cease Membership & Committees Reconstituted
item.description:
Hexaware Technologies Ltd has fixed the date of its 33rd Annual General Meeting (AGM) on May 05, 2026, through video conferencing/other audio-visual means. The company also announced that Mr. Milind Sarwate, Independent Director, will cease to be a Director of the Company on April 24, 2026, upon completion of his second consecutive term. Mr. Alok Chandra Misra has been appointed as his successor. The Board has also approved the reconstitution of committees, with Mr. Alok Chandra Misra appointed as the Chairman of the Audit Committee and Environmental, Social & Governance Committee, and Ms. Sukanya Kripalu as the Chairperson of the Nomination & Remuneration Committee.

  • AGM date fixed for May 05, 2026
  • Director Milind Sarwate to cease membership on April 24, 2026
  • Alok Chandra Misra appointed as successor to Milind Sarwate
  • Reconstitution of Audit Committee and Environmental, Social & Governance Committee
  • Sukanya Kripalu appointed as Chairperson of the Nomination & Remuneration Committee

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hexaware Technologies Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [PANELEC] Pan Electronics India Ltd Repays Loan from Punjab National Bank
item.description:
Pan Electronics India Ltd has repaid the loan amount obtained from Punjab National Bank. The original loan amount was Three Crore, which was modified to Rupees 71 Lakhs Five thousand Only on 18.07.2025. The loan was fully repaid on 02-04-2026. The loan was secured by a charge on entire Current Assets such as Stock, Book Debts and Raw Materials for Cash Credit, and Hypothecation of Machineries Purchased for Term Loan. The repayment of this loan is not expected to have a material impact on the company's financial or operational aspects.

  • Pan Electronics India Ltd repaid loan from Punjab National Bank
  • Original loan amount was Three Crore, modified to Rupees 71 Lakhs Five thousand Only
  • Loan was secured by a charge on Current Assets and Hypothecation of Machineries
  • Loan was fully repaid on 02-04-2026
  • Repayment of loan is not expected to have a material impact on the company's financial or operational aspects

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: PAN ELECTRONICS INDIA LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [CLEANMAX] Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd Completes Early Redemption of INR 499 Crore NCDs
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd has completed the early/pre-mature redemption of Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) worth INR 499 Crore. The NCDs, having a face value of INR 10 Lakh each, were issued on a private placement basis. The company confirmed timely payment of both interest and principal as per Regulation 57 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations 2015.

  • Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd has completed the early/pre-mature redemption of INR 499 Crore NCDs.
  • The NCDs were issued on a private placement basis and had a face value of INR 10 Lakh each.
  • The company confirmed timely payment of both interest and principal as per Regulation 57 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations 2015.
  • The redemption is a part of the company's debt management strategy.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd | Category: Buyback

item.title: [NXST] Nexus Select Trust Subscribes to INR 115 Crores NCDs of Runwal Residency Private Limited
item.description:
Nexus Select Trust, managed by Nexus Select Mall Management Private Limited, has subscribed to redeemable, unlisted, unrated, secured, non-convertible debentures (NCDs) aggregating to INR 115 Crores issued by Runwal Residency Private Limited. This decision was approved in a Board Meeting held on February 23, 2026, and the Trust has directly and/or through its SPVs/Holdcos subscribed to the NCDs on April 02, 2026. The NCDs are secured against second ranking security.

  • Nexus Select Trust subscribes to INR 115 Crores NCDs of Runwal Residency Private Limited
  • The NCDs are secured against second ranking security
  • The decision was approved in a Board Meeting held on February 23, 2026
  • The Trust has directly and/or through its SPVs/Holdcos subscribed to the NCDs on April 02, 2026
  • Runwal Residency Private Limited is a real estate company

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Nexus Select Trust | Category: Investment

item.title: [3IINFOTECH] 3i Infotech Secures Rs. 2.04 Crore Order from Leading Private Sector Bank for Digitization Project
item.description:
3i Infotech Ltd has received an order from a leading private sector bank in India for undertaking a digitization and document management project for their legacy records of credit card applications. The contract is rate card-based, with an approximate value of Rs. 2.04 Crore, exclusive of applicable taxes. The project involves inventory preparation, data entry, barcoding, scanning, indexing, storage, retrieval, and shredding of legacy credit card application records. The contract is awarded for a period of 12 months.

  • 3i Infotech secures order from a leading private sector bank for digitization project
  • Project involves inventory preparation, data entry, barcoding, scanning, indexing, storage, retrieval, and shredding of legacy credit card application records
  • Rate card-based contract with an approximate value of Rs. 2.04 Crore
  • Contract awarded for a period of 12 months
  • Project aims to enhance visibility, accessibility, and compliance of historical customer records while ensuring secure data processing and adherence to regulatory and audit requirements

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: 3I INFOTECH LTD. | Category: New Launch

item.title: [UNIONBANK] Union Bank of India Reports 9.76% YoY Growth in Total Gross Advances as of 31.03.2026
item.description:
Union Bank of India has reported a 9.76% YoY growth in Total Gross Advances as of 31st March 2026. Total Domestic Advances grew by 10.06% YoY and RAM Advances (Domestic) grew by 12.59% YoY during the same period. Domestic CASA Deposits grew by 7.90% YoY and 10.82% QoQ as of 31st March 2026.

  • Union Bank of India reports 9.76% YoY growth in Total Gross Advances as of 31.03.2026
  • Total Domestic Advances grew by 10.06% YoY
  • RAM Advances (Domestic) grew by 12.59% YoY
  • Domestic CASA Deposits grew by 7.90% YoY and 10.82% QoQ as of 31st March 2026

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: UNION BANK OF INDIA | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [LEMONTREE] Lemon Tree Hotels Expands Presence in Nepal with New Signing in Simara
item.description:
Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd, one of India’s leading hospitality chains, has signed a License Agreement for Lemon Tree Hotel, Simara, Nepal. This will be the brand's 7th property in Nepal, managed by Carnation Hotels Private Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lemon Tree Hotels Limited. The hotel will feature 98 well-appointed rooms, a restaurant, a banquet, conference room, other F & B venues like bar lounge and recreational facilities including swimming pool, spa, and fitness centre. The hotel is strategically located just 2 km from Simara Airport, serving as a vital bridge between the region’s industrial strength and its ecological treasures.

  • Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd expands its presence in Nepal with a new signing in Simara.
  • The hotel will be managed by Carnation Hotels Private Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lemon Tree Hotels Limited.
  • The hotel will feature 98 well-appointed rooms, a restaurant, a banquet, conference room, other F & B venues, and recreational facilities.
  • The hotel is strategically located just 2 km from Simara Airport.
  • This is the brand's 7th property in Nepal.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd | Category: New Launch
